data.title: Looking for tariff-resistant stocks? Think sports technology names
data.description:
Sportradar Group AG (NASDAQ:SRAD) broke to its highest level in more than three years as the sports data company continues to attract investors looking for a company that does not face tariff headwinds.  At the company's investor day event last month, CEO Carsten Koerl and the management team highlighted the company's competitive advantages and its position at a pivotal moment for multi-year value creation. Sportradar (NASDAQ:SRAD) emphasized that its unmatched scale, extensive sports rights, and innovative product portfolio are key drivers for sustainable revenue growth, margin expansion, and cash generation.

Sportradar (SRAD) also released financial targets, including expectations for 2027 revenue of €1.7 billion in revenue, adjusted EBITDA of €455 million, a 700-basis-point margin expansion, and €275 million in free cash flow with at least 60% conversion.

On Wall Street, 12 out of 14 firms covering the sports data stock have a Buy-equivalent rating. Seeking Alpha analysts also lean bullish in general. Redfox Capital Ideas wrote recently that "SRAD's structural moat is strong, the upsell opportunity is clear, and execution on expanding internationally has been solid."

Shares of Sportradar (SRAD) are up 36% on a year-to-date basis, while peer Genius Sports (NYSE:GENI) is 18% higher.

Genius Sports (NYSE:GENI) stands out by having a Seeking Alpha Quant Rating of Strong Buy and one of the highest quant scores in the consumer discretionary sector. Recently, B. Riley Securities started off coverage on GENI with a Buy rating, noting its live sports data is deeply integrated into 650+ sportsbooks with long-term contracts that provide visibility and growth as it adds more leagues and events to the portfolio.

data.title: Prologis outlines strategic focus amid Q1 2025 performance and tariff challenges
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Prologis (PLD) Q1 2025

Management View

  • The company reported leasing 58 million square feet during the quarter, achieving near-record levels. New developments worth $650 million were initiated, with 80% being build-to-suit projects averaging 16-year lease terms.
  • Core FFO per share was $1.42 including net promotes and $1.43 excluding net promotes, surpassing prior forecasts.
  • Occupancy stood at 95.2%, reflecting a 70 basis point decline from year-end, attributed to seasonal factors and strong retention.
  • CFO Tim Arndt highlighted that net effective rent change was 54%, with cash basis rent change at 32%. Same-store growth was 5.9% on a net effective basis and 6.2% on a cash basis for the quarter.
  • Managing Director Chris Caton indicated that power capacity for data center developments expanded by 400 megawatts, with plans to report additional activity in the next quarter.
  • The company secured an A2 credit rating from Moody’s, positioning Prologis as one of two public REITs with an 'A flat' rating from both agencies.

Outlook

  • Core FFO guidance remains between $5.65 and $5.81 per share including net promote expense, and $5.70 to $5.86 per share excluding it.
  • Development start guidance was revised down to $1.5 billion to $2 billion, reflecting reduced expectations for speculative projects amid market uncertainty.
  • Capital deployment guidance for combined contributions and dispositions decreased to $400 million to $1 billion, driven by cautious views on market conditions.
  • G&A guidance increased to $450 million to $470 million, partly due to impacts from lower development activity.
  • Management emphasized long-term resilience and readiness to adjust to market shifts, stating: "A disconnected world will require more warehouse space, not less" (CFO Tim Arndt).

Financial Results

  • Net effective lease mark-to-market ended the quarter at 25%, representing $1.1 billion of incremental NOI potential.
  • Over $900 million in solar and storage capacity was either operational or under development, advancing towards a 1-gigawatt goal for the year.
  • Capital raised in flagship open-ended funds was approximately $400 million, with redemptions leaving a neutral balance.
  • $550 million in debt was issued during the quarter at a weighted average rate of 4.1%, including a Canadian bond transaction.

Q&A

  • Tom Catherwood, BTIG, asked about lessons from past disruptions and their relevance to the current environment. CEO Hamid Moghadam responded that consumption trends remained robust historically, even during tariff implementations, and emphasized the importance of geographic diversification.
  • Steve Sakwa, Evercore ISI, queried about occupancy declines and future forecasts. CFO Tim Arndt attributed the decline to a seasonal increase in lease expirations and highlighted expectations for occupancy to stabilize later in the year.
  • Michael Goldsmith, UBS, asked about Amazon's market reentry's potential impact. President Dan Letter confirmed notable leasing activity with Amazon, contributing to a broader resurgence in e-commerce-related leasing.
  • Vikram Malhotra, Mizuho, inquired about stress test assumptions related to market downturns. CFO Tim Arndt detailed that the company modeled scenarios based on historical crises, including significant rent and occupancy declines, to ensure the resilience of earnings guidance.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts exhibited cautious optimism, focusing on leasing trends, tariff impacts, and data center growth. Questions reflected concerns about macroeconomic uncertainties and their influence on Prologis' performance.
  • Management conveyed confidence in the company’s strategy and readiness to navigate challenges. CEO Hamid Moghadam emphasized the adaptability of supply chains and the long-term demand for logistics spaces.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for development starts was reduced to $1.5 billion-$2 billion, compared to $2.25 billion-$2.75 billion targeted in Q4 2024. Meanwhile, occupancy decreased from 95.8%, reflecting seasonal trends.
  • Leasing activity decreased from the record 60 million square feet in Q4 2024 to 58 million in Q1 2025.
  • Net effective rent changes declined from 66% in Q4 2024 to 54% in Q1 2025.
  • Data center initiatives progressed, with 400 MW of power added, compared to 1.4 GW of secured power mentioned in the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged uncertainties stemming from global tariffs and potential disruptions to logistics and supply chains, stating that customers are adapting with flexible inventory strategies and short-term solutions.
  • Analysts raised concerns regarding the pace of leasing activity and the potential for delays in customer decision-making due to macroeconomic uncertainties.

Final Takeaway

Prologis emphasized its resilience and ability to capitalize on market opportunities, despite tariff-related uncertainties and evolving customer needs. While revising certain guidance metrics downward, the company remains focused on long-term growth through strategic investments and leveraging its diversified global footprint.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Fulton financial outlines 2025 guidance with focus on low-single digit loan growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Fulton Financial Corporation (FULT) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Curtis Myers, Chairman and CEO, highlighted operating earnings per share of $0.52 for the quarter, a $0.04 increase compared to the previous quarter. Revenue exceeded expectations, while total operating expenses decreased, leading to a quarterly operating efficiency ratio of 56.7%. Operating return on assets reached 1.25%, and operating return on average tangible common equity was reported at 15.95%.
  • Myers elaborated on the continued progress of the FultonFirst initiative, which focuses on talent alignment, reinvestment for growth, and operational simplification. He noted improvements in liquidity and equity, with tangible book value per share growing 13.8% on an annualized basis.
  • Customer deposits increased by $200 million, driven by growth in interest-bearing money market products, while total loans declined by $182 million primarily due to strategic decisions involving indirect auto balances and commercial construction loans.
  • CFO Richard Kraemer reported that net interest income was $251 million, with a net interest margin of 3.43%, a 2-basis-point increase from the previous quarter. Loan yields declined by 11 basis points to 5.86%, while the average cost of total deposits decreased by 11 basis points to 2.03%.

Outlook

  • Management confirmed its operating guidance for 2025, considering an updated forecast of four 25-basis-point cuts to the Fed funds rate beginning in June. They maintained guidance ranges for net interest income and non-interest income but noted that these metrics could trend toward the lower end due to a potential slower growth environment.
  • Loan growth is anticipated to be in the low-single-digit range for the year, reflecting cautious optimism amid ongoing macroeconomic uncertainties.

Financial Results

  • Operating net income available to common shareholders was $95.5 million for Q1 2025. Non-interest income was $67.2 million, while operating expenses decreased by $7.8 million compared to the previous quarter.
  • Provision expense decreased by $2.8 million quarter-over-quarter to $13.9 million, with the allowance for credit losses to total loans increasing to 1.59%. The non-performing loan coverage ratio rose to 193%.
  • Deposit growth was primarily attributed to interest-bearing products, offset by declines in municipal and brokered deposits. The loan-to-deposit ratio declined to 91%.

Q&A

  • Frank Schiraldi of Piper Sandler asked about loan growth expectations. Curtis Myers responded that the pipeline remains steady, but loan growth depends on customer confidence and project pull-through.
  • Daniel Tamayo of Raymond James inquired about credit quality and potential impacts from tariffs. Myers stated that the agricultural portfolio, valued at $1 billion, is being monitored closely, along with manufacturing and auto dealer segments.
  • Andrew Leischner of KBW questioned the timing of share buybacks. Myers explained that buybacks remain a third priority after organic growth and corporate initiatives.
  • Matthew Breese of Stephens asked about net interest income sensitivity to rate cuts. Kraemer estimated a 25-basis-point cut would result in a $1.7 million annual headwind.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts exhibited cautious sentiment, focusing on loan growth, credit quality, and rate sensitivity. Questions often sought clarity on potential headwinds in key areas like deposit costs and credit risks.
  • Management maintained a slightly optimistic tone, emphasizing operational efficiencies and strategic initiatives. Prepared remarks were more confident, whereas responses during Q&A reflected a cautious approach to uncertainties.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone was consistent, emphasizing stability and strategic focus.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Operating earnings per share increased by $0.04 from Q4 2024, driven by reduced operating expenses and stable revenue.
  • Net interest margin improved by 2 basis points, reversing the 8-basis-point decline from the previous quarter.
  • Loan balances continued to decline, with the pace of reduction accelerating from $131 million in Q4 2024 to $182 million in Q1 2025, reflecting ongoing strategic repositioning.
  • Management sentiment remained steady, focusing on operational efficiency and cautious growth amid macroeconomic challenges.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited potential headwinds from macroeconomic conditions, including tariff impacts and customer hesitancy in project execution.
  • Credit quality improvements were noted, but risks remain, particularly in agriculture and manufacturing portfolios.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the impact of rate cuts on net interest income and the sustainability of deposit cost management.

Final Takeaway

Fulton Financial Corporation started 2025 with solid financial results and operational efficiencies driven by the FultonFirst initiative. While management remains cautious about macroeconomic challenges and credit risks, they reaffirmed their guidance and strategic focus on customer growth, operational simplification, and prudent balance sheet management. Investors should monitor the company’s ability to achieve low-single-digit loan growth and manage potential headwinds from rate cuts and deposit pricing pressures.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ECB set to cut rates again as Trump’s tariff looms over economic growth
data.description:
The European Central Bank (ECB) will announce its interest rate decision on Thursday, with market expectations leaning towards a 25-basis point cut triggered by Trump’s tariff announcement, bringing the policy rate down to 2.25% from 2.50%.

This would mark the second-biggest central bank's seventh rate reduction since it began easing monetary policy in June 2024.

With euro’s recent appreciation against dollar and decreasing inflation in Euro Area, market projections suggest the ECB may also implement rate cut during its June meeting as global trade and market turmoil induced by President Trump’s tariffs announcement brought back growth concerns for Eurozone.

The ZEW Indicator of Economic Sentiment for Germany sank to -14 in April 2025, the lowest since July 2023, and sentiment for the Euro Area slumped by 58.3 points from the prior month, suggesting that economic sentiment is dampening in the Europe.

“The ongoing trade tensions as well as a high level of uncertainty could force the ECB to cut interest rates further than it would currently like to admit,” said Carsten Brzeski, Global Head of Macro, ING Research.

However, President Trump's announcement to put a 90-day pause on tariffs placed on countries, except China, can open the gate for negotiations.

Meanwhile, a key area of focus is whether the ECB is prepared to accept the increasing divergence between its monetary policy stance and that of the U.S. Federal Reserve. As Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell, at the Society for Advancing Business Editing and Writing annual conference in Arlington, Va, stressed that “the central bank doesn't "need to be in a hurry" and that it was not clear what the appropriate path of monetary policy should be.”

With the rate cut largely priced in, attention will turn to the ECB’s press conference, where President Christine Lagarde’s remarks will be closely scrutinized. Investors will be looking for any commentary on the potential impact of tariffs and ECB’s future policy.

data.title: Harley-Davidson defends its CEO transition plan
data.description:
Harley-Davidson (NYSE:HOG) provided an update to the letter sent by H Partners to shareholders earlier in the day, in which the independent investment firm called for the removal of the motorcycle manufacturer's CEO and a reshuffling of the board of directors.

Statement provided to Seeking Alpha:

"We are disappointed that H Partners has chosen to take this self-serving action targeting members of our board after that firm’s preferred CEO candidate failed to receive majority support from the Board’s Independent Directors. H Partners has chosen to put its own interest ahead of the interests of other shareholders by attempting to disrupt the Board’s rigorous and thoughtful CEO transition process, creating uncertainty for and putting Harley-Davidson’s future and shareholder value at risk. The Board is committed to acting in the interests of all of Harley-Davidson’s shareholders by continuing to strengthen the Company’s foundation for the future and selecting the right CEO to lead Harley-Davidson into its next chapter."

Shares of Harley-Davidson (NYSE:HOG) were up 1.4% at 2:30 p.m. on volume above normal activity. The company is expected to report Q1 earnings sometime in the next two weeks, based on the timing of the report over the last few years.

data.title: Crypto exchange OKX expands to U.S., sets up regional headquarters in California
data.description:
Seychelles-based cryptocurrency exchange OKX is expanding to the U.S. and establishing new regional headquarters in San Jose, California, said U.S. Chief Executive Officer Roshan Robert in a Wednesday statement.

Customers located in the U.S. now have access to OKX's centralized trading platform and wallet offering, he said, noting new features will be rolled out throughout the year "as part of our vision to build a crypto Super App."

"Our entry into America is more than a market expansion—it’s a commitment to responsible growth," the newly-appointed CEO noted. 

The move comes as the U.S.'s crypto market gains more traction amid President Donald Trump's support for crypto-friendly policies.

“As regulations evolve, OKX is working closely with US regulators and policymakers to ensure we operate transparently and compliantly,” Robert said. “We’ve built a comprehensive, risk-based global compliance program that includes enhanced due diligence, a robust KYC process, customer risk rating systems, advanced fraud detection, AML tools, geo-blocking, and market surveillance technologies. These are all part of our commitment to a secure, compliant trading environment.”

It was only two months ago when OKcoin, the U.S. division of OKX, resolved allegations of operating in the U.S. without a money transmitting license, agreeing to pay over $500M in fines to the Justice Department.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Nasdaq slides 3.5%, Dow sheds 600 points as hawkish Powell adds to tariff angst
data.description:
Wall Street on Wednesday extended its steep losses after Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell expressed concerns about the likely higher-than-anticipated effects from tariffs, including possibly putting pressure on the central bank's dual mandate of stable prices and strong employment. 

Sentiment had earlier taken a beating after some negative updates from the world's biggest semiconductor companies ignited fresh trade war concerns and sent technology stocks spiraling.

The benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) was last -2.34% at 5,270.52 points in late afternoon trade, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -3.53% at 16,229.67 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -1.57% at 39,733.85 points.

Powell, appearing at an event at the Economic Club of Chicago, said in prepared remarks that tariffs were "highly likely to generate at least a temporary rise in inflation." In the subsequent discussion, the central bank chief said policymakers were looking to "make sure" tariffs only caused a one-time increase in prices.    

"Let me translate: the Fed is not expecting to 'look through tariffs.' Quite the opposite is the takeaway here. The text as well as the Q&A read & sound hawkish as a resolute Powell prepares for a disruption to the economy," Joseph Brusuelas, principal and chief economist at RSM US LLP, said on X (formerly Twitter).

Powell was also asked about the turmoil in markets, to which the Fed chair said markets were "struggling with a lot of uncertainty" and "processing historically unique developments." But he also said markets were "orderly" and functioning as expected.   

Looking at the 11 S&P sectors, nine were now in the red. Technology shed -4.6%, topping the losers.

Nvidia (NVDA) slid -9.3% and was the top percentage loser on both the S&P and Nasdaq. The world's third-largest company by market cap on Tuesday said it had been informed by the U.S. government that it would require a special license - indefinitely - to ship its H20 graphic processing units and related hardware to China. 

NVDA rival AMD (AMD) fell -8.4% and was also among the top percentage losers on the S&P and Nasdaq. It also informed of a new license requirement to ship certain products to China. Both NVDA and AMD expect to take charges related to these export controls. 

Meanwhile, Dutch chip giant ASML (ASML) retreated -7.2% after reporting quarterly bookings that missed estimates. The company also cautioned that tariffs were increasing uncertainty for the long term.

Turning to Wednesday's economic calendar, the spotlight was on the March retail sales report. The headline figure improved +1.4% M/M, while the core figure came in at +0.5%. 

Over in the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were lower. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was last down 1 basis point to 4.31%. The shorter-end more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was also down 1 basis point to 3.82%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

data.title: Siemens Energy hikes full-year guidance after stronger than expected Q2 profit
data.description:
Siemens Energy (OTCPK:SMEGF) +5.3% in Wednesday's trading after raising its outlook for FY 2025 after enjoying a strong performance in all of its business areas during its FQ2.

The company said it now expects a profit margin before special items of 4%-6% for the full year, up from prior guidance of 3%-5%, while foreseeing a 13%-15% boost in sales, up from 8%-10% previously, while pre-tax free cash flow pre-tax is expected at ~€4B ($4.55B).

Citing a "positive business development" without providing more context, Siemens Energy's (OTCPK:SMEGF) Q2 profit before special items surged more than 5x to €906M, for a 9.1% profit margin, easily exceeding the 6.2% analyst consensus estimate, as the company benefits from rising global demand for power.

data.title: OpenAI in talks to acquire Windsurf for $3B: report
data.description:
• OpenAI is in talks to acquire artificial intelligence-assisted coding tool Windsurf for about $3B, Bloomberg reported.
  • The acquisition, which would be OpenAI's largest, is still ongoing and terms could change, the news outlet added, citing a person familiar with the matter. The deal could also fall apart, the person added.
  • If it goes through, Windsurf could help OpenAI boost its capabilities against Anthropic, GitHub — owned by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) — and other AI coding tools.
  • OpenAI, which is backed by Microsoft, did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • OpenAI recently closed a new funding round that values the ChatGPT maker at $300B.

data.title: IPO watch: Aerospace and defense company AIRO is going public this week
data.description:
AIRO Group Holdings (AIRO) is expected to launch its initial public offering on April 17. The company is an advanced aerospace and defense technology company based in Albuquerque, New Mexico.

AIRO (AIRO) operates across four main business segments, which are drones (uncrewed air systems), avionics, commercial and military aviation training, and electric and hybrid vertical lift aircraft, including electric Vertical Take Off and Landing (or eVTOL) vehicles. AIRO’s (AIRO) offerings are focused on both commercial and government markets, leveraging proprietary technologies and long-standing relationships with the U.S. government and NATO countries to provide integrated solutions in aerospace, urban air mobility, and autonomy. 

In its SEC S-1 filing, AIRO (AIRO) said that it intends to launch U.S. production of its military drones and subsequently seek DoD Blue UAS drone certification, which it currently estimates will take approximately six months to achieve and will allow it to sell drones to the DoD. The certification process involves sponsors in various U.S. military branches supporting the product, with full certification essentially contingent on U.S.-domiciled production. 

For the year ended December 31, 2024, the Albuquerque, New Mexico-based company reported $86.94 million in revenue, reflecting a 101% growth rate from the prior year. 

The IPO of 5 million shares is expected to be between $14.00 and $16.00 per share. AIRO (AIRO) expects to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 750,000 shares (solely to cover over-allotments, if any) of its common stock at the initial public offering price, less underwriting discounts and commissions. The company currently intends to use the net proceeds it receives from the offering, together with its existing cash and restricted cash to support growth initiatives across each segment, repay debt, and for general corporate purposes.
